evals.name: transcribe-audio-whisper
evals.prompt: I have a public audio recording at https://dare.wisc.edu/wp-content/uploads/sites/1051/2008/04/Arthur.mp3 . Using aiXplain, transcribe it to text and also tell me which language it detected. Give me a short Python script and the actual transcript.
assertions: Uses the aiXplain v2 SDK (from aixplain import Aixplain)
Uses the Whisper model (66311fda6eb563279c574b71) via aix.Model.get + run with source_audio
Actually executes and returns a real transcript ('The story of Arthur the Rat')
Reports the detected language as English
Provides a runnable Python script

evals.name: rag-agent-over-docs
evals.prompt: Build me an aiXplain agent that answers questions from our product catalog. Here are the products: 1) AeroBook laptop, $1200, electronics, 14in OLED; 2) PulseBuds earbuds, $89, electronics, noise cancelling; 3) TrailPack backpack, $65, outdoor, 30L. Ingest these into a knowledge base, wire up an agent over it, deploy it, and then ask it 'what electronics do you have under $100?' to prove it works.
assertions: Creates an aiR vector index Tool with integration 6904bcf672a6e36b68bb72fb
Upserts the three products into the index
Scopes the index tool to read-only (search/get) before attaching
Builds and saves (deploys) an agent that searches the index
Answer correctly identifies PulseBuds ($89) and excludes the AeroBook ($1200)
Shares a Studio link or deployed agent id

evals.name: web-research-agent-deploy
evals.prompt: Create and deploy an aiXplain agent that can research current topics on the web and cite its sources. Then run it on 'What are the latest developments in fusion energy?' and show me the answer plus how much it cost.
assertions: Finds/uses a web search tool via marketplace search
Scopes the tool's allowed_actions
Builds and saves (deploys) the agent -> ONBOARDED
Runs the query and returns a substantive cited answer
Reports the cost (execution_stats credits)
Shares a Studio link or agent id

evals.name: governed-team-agent
evals.prompt: I need a customer-support agent for aiXplain that NEVER reveals internal pricing margins. Set it up as a team agent with a runtime guardrail (inspector) that blocks any response mentioning internal margins or markup, and demonstrate the guardrail firing on the question 'what is your internal markup on deployed agents?'.
assertions: Builds a team agent with at least one subagent
Attaches an Inspector from aixplain.v2.inspector targeting output with a blocking/correcting action
Saves the team with save_subcomponents=True -> ONBOARDED
Runs the adversarial query and demonstrates the guard without leaking the markup
Uses only real SDK inspector classes (no fabricated API)
